goal: 让 OpenNori 以 agent-native-cli Architecture Baseline 完成 Architecture Baseline Loop 产品化能力和后续架构修复
summary: Use a thin deterministic CLI, domain-first modules, schema-backed project state, manifest-managed lifecycle actions, agent-readable Skills, and build-vs-buy checks before custom infrastructure.
applies_to: Skill Pack and manifest managed tools

sources.label: CodeGraph / GitNexus
sources.lesson: Expose deterministic agent-readable context, status, doctor, and staleness signals without making OpenNori a code graph platform.

sources.label: Compound Engineering Plugin
sources.lesson: Keep command, parser, converter/target, and Skill surfaces separate without adopting process-centered multi-agent review.

sources.label: ECC
sources.lesson: Use manifest/profile/component/install-state style productization without becoming a workflow OS.

sources.label: CLI-Anything
sources.lesson: Treat CLI, Skill, tests, and registry-like discoverability as product assets without becoming a generic harness market.

sources.label: vibecode-pro-max-kit
sources.lesson: Borrow managed-file install and Skill distribution boundaries, but reject RIPER phase folders and process/ as the mainline.

checks.statement: Opening the CLI entry should make it clear that command parsing, dispatch, and output are separate from domain decisions.
checks.review: Inspect CLI entry, command handlers, and domain modules before claiming architecture complete.

checks.statement: Changing Product AC discovery should not require editing installer, Skill Pack, manifest, or report rendering logic.
Install, upgrade, uninstall, managed files, and doctor recovery are lifecycle concerns with dry-run preview and explicit confirmation boundaries.
checks.review: Inspect lifecycle commands, manifest state, and doctor output.

checks.statement: Before custom infrastructure work, check current project dependencies, standard libraries, official SDKs, mature open-source libraries, and documented reference projects.
checks.review: Record a build-vs-buy decision or challenge before self-building infrastructure.

checks.statement: Do not replace this baseline silently. Raise an Architecture Challenge when project evidence conflicts with it.

preferred_libraries.policy: Prefer a mature CLI parser or current project convention over growing custom argument parsing.
Prefer schema validation libraries or JSON Schema over long-term handwritten schema validation.
Prefer established markdown/frontmatter libraries when parsing becomes non-trivial; keep OpenNori-specific rendering local when it is product domain logic.

avoid: phase/task-list state as product completion state
agent self-summary as the only high-risk completion evidence
